page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is an Edsv6 family virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor featuring 24 physical cores with hyper-threading running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction support such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning and cryptography acceleration. Local storage is bundled with a 2835 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the E48ds_v6 demonstrates strong performance across multiple compute and memory-bound tasks. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench image processing and strong ratings in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform well, with strong scores in Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded Gzip compression. However, a notable architectural tradeoff is observed in memory performance: while small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is strong, the Passmark memory score falls into the weak category, and large-block uncached read and write operations remain average.

Given its memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core and substantial local storage, the E48ds_v6 offers high resource density for memory-intensive applications. It is highly capable for database operations, as evidenced by strong Redis SET throughput and average-to-strong database benchmarks. The inclusion of Intel AMX enables strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference for small and medium models, though large 70B model text generation drops to average speeds. This profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for enterprise databases, memory-cached web serving, software compilation pipelines, and moderate scale AI inference workloads that do not require dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor at 4.2 GHz. It includes 24 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and a bundled 2835 GB local disk. Benchmarks show strong performance in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and image processing, alongside strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities powered by Intel AMX. However, while cached memory bandwidth is strong, Passmark memory scores indicate a performance tradeoff in uncached memory operations. With its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local storage, this instance is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and enterprise web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is an Edsv6 virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 2835 GB local disk, delivering strong database, compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 features 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and advanced Intel matrix extensions for demanding database and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed.
384.0 GB RAM providing a high-density 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio.
Bundled 2835 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Intel AMX support delivering strong CPU-based LLM inference performance.
Strong multi-core compute, software compilation, and image processing benchmarks.
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and enterprise web serving.
